capability_objective: The logistics desk is reconciling two shipments for the exception board. The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale matches. Find the shipment named “Seabird Training Kits” at “San Diego Hub” and the shipment named “Redwood Archive Boxes” at “Sacramento Hub”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Give each record one sentence in the requested order, then add exactly one final sentence comparing the statuses; do not add recommendations.
prompt:
The logistics desk is reconciling two shipments for the exception board. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable shipment registry documented in README.md through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing store, or its audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness.

The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale or partial matches. Find the shipment named “Seabird Training Kits” at “San Diego Hub” and the shipment named “Redwood Archive Boxes” at “Sacramento Hub”. Every reported value must be grounded through the registry: search by the exact name and location first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that same branch. Treat the branches as independent. In the first shipment-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, one search per call; do not combine the operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both searches to return. Retrieve a branch only if its own search produced exactly one nonempty stable ID. When both branches resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, one get per Pi Bash tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get, never from search output.

This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the registry, its backing data, its audit trail, or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete registry audit events. Leave no scratch artifacts in the workspace.

Write the complete result to exception_board.md and no other new file. The file must contain exactly three newline-terminated sentences, one per line. The first two sentences must cover the requested shipments in the requested order. For a resolved branch, use `<name> at <location> has status <status>.`, preserving the status exactly as returned by its complete record. For an unresolved branch, use `<name> at <location> is unresolved.` When both statuses were retrieved, make the final sentence exactly `The statuses match.` or `The statuses differ.`, as appropriate. Otherwise make it exactly `A status comparison is unavailable because at least one shipment is unresolved.` Add no heading, bullet, label, blank line, note, recommendation, evidence, or unrelated match. After writing the file, return exactly the same content as the final response.
